skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: Use this skill when the user wants to produce a short video (5-120 seconds). Supports any video type: product ads, TikTok/Instagram/YouTube content, brand videos, explainers, social clips. USE FOR: video production, AI video, make a video, product video, brand video, promotional clip, explainer video, short video.
skill.summary: AI-powered video creation skill that produces short videos (5-120 seconds) through the Pexo platform.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate AI video production CLI tool that interacts with the Pexo API. The static analyzer flagged 534 patterns across 13 files, but most are false positives from standard shell scripting conventions. All network requests target the declared Pexo API endpoint (pexo.ai). Shell commands use safe argument construction via jq --arg (no injection vectors). The credential handling follows standard API key patterns. No malicious behavior, data exfiltration, or unauthorized access was found. Users should be aware the skill requires network access to an external API, stores API keys in a local config file, and executes shell scripts.

medium_findings.title: External API dependency with credential handling
medium_findings.description: The skill makes network requests to an external API (pexo.ai) using a bearer token API key. The API key is stored in a plaintext config file at ~/.pexo/config. This is a standard pattern but users should understand their credentials are transmitted to a third-party service.

medium_findings.title: Shell script execution with external process invocations
medium_findings.description: The skill contains 9 shell scripts that invoke external commands (curl, jq, file, stat, mktemp, date, sed, awk, tr). These are standard Unix utilities used for HTTP requests, JSON processing, and file handling. Arguments are constructed safely using jq --arg, preventing shell injection.

medium_findings.title: Configuration file stored in home directory
medium_findings.description: The skill reads and sources a configuration file at ~/.pexo/config which contains the API key and base URL. This is documented to the user. The file is sourced (not just read), which means it executes any content in the file. Users should ensure this file only contains the expected environment variable assignments.

low_findings.title: Static analyzer flagged patterns are false positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 534 patterns including external_commands (349), network (27), filesystem (79), and env_access (25). All flagged patterns are standard shell scripting conventions: command substitution $(...), curl HTTP requests to declared endpoints, temp file creation with mktemp, and environment variable reads for configuration. No actual security vulnerabilities were confirmed.

content.user_title: Create AI-generated short videos with Pexo
content.value_statement: Creating professional videos requires specialized skills in cinematography, editing, and motion design. This skill connects you to Pexo, an AI video creation platform that handles all creative work automatically from your description.
actual_capabilities: Create short videos (5-120 seconds) in multiple aspect ratios (16:9, 9:16, 1:1)
Upload images, videos, and audio files as reference material
Poll for video production status and handle preview selections
Manage video projects including creation, revision, and delivery
Check account credit balance and handle credit-related errors
Support multiple video types: product ads, TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, explainers
limitations: Requires a Pexo API key and account with available credits
Video production takes 15-20 minutes or longer for complex videos
Cannot crawl web URLs; files must be downloaded locally before uploading
Supported file types are limited to common image, video, and audio formats
Each message to Pexo costs tokens; messages should be consolidated

use_cases.title: Social media content creation
use_cases.description: A social media manager needs quick video content for TikTok, Instagram, or YouTube. They describe the video concept and the skill handles production through Pexo.

use_cases.title: Product marketing videos
use_cases.description: A small business owner wants to create product advertisement videos. They upload product photos and describe the desired video, then receive a polished promotional clip.

use_cases.title: Explainer video production
use_cases.description: An educator or trainer needs short explainer videos for their course. They describe the topic and Pexo generates the video with appropriate visuals and pacing.

prompt_templates.title: Create your first video
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to create a short video. Help me set up Pexo and create my first video.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user who has not used the skill before

prompt_templates.title: Video with uploaded image
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a video using this product photo as reference. Upload the image and send it to Pexo.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a local image file they want to use as reference

prompt_templates.title: Revise an existing video
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to revise the video in project {project_id}. Tell Pexo to make the background brighter and add upbeat music.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to modify a previously created video

prompt_templates.title: Check project status and credits
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the status of my Pexo project and tell me how many credits I have remaining.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to monitor project progress and account balance

output_examples.input: Create a 30-second product video for my running shoes
output: Your video request has been submitted to Pexo.
Video production is in progress (estimated 10 minutes remaining).
Preview: Pexo has shared 3 video options (A, B, C).
Please review and select your preferred option.

best_practices: Upload reference files before sending your creative brief to Pexo for better results.
Consolidate all information into a single message to Pexo to minimize token costs.
Monitor the polling loop and respond promptly when Pexo asks clarifying questions or shows previews.
anti_patterns: Do not add creative details like duration, aspect ratio, or style that the user did not specify. Let Pexo make those decisions.
Do not call pexo-chat.sh during WAIT status. This triggers duplicate video production and wastes credits.
Do not send bare asset IDs in messages. Always wrap them in the correct tags like <original-image>asset_id</original-image>.

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a Pexo account with an API key (starting with sk-) and available credits. Install the skill, run pexo-doctor.sh to verify your setup, and configure your API key in ~/.pexo/config.

faq.question: How long does video production take?
faq.answer: A typical 15-second video takes about 15-20 minutes. Longer or more complex videos may take additional time. The skill polls every 60 seconds and provides progress updates.

faq.question: What file formats can I upload?
faq.answer: Images: jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic. Videos: mp4, mov, avi. Audio: mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac.

faq.question: What happens when my credits run out?
faq.answer: When credits are insufficient, the skill will display a purchase link and instructions. Visit pexo.ai/home, click Credits, and purchase additional credits. After adding credits, the failed step can be retried.

faq.question: Can I revise a video after it is created?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the same project_id and send your revision feedback through pexo-chat.sh. The skill will handle the revision workflow just like the initial creation.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: Videos are produced in aspect ratios of 16:9 (landscape), 9:16 (portrait/vertical), or 1:1 (square). Duration ranges from 5 to 120 seconds. Output files are delivered as downloadable video files with signed URLs.
